description: This rule configures the system to use default pam_tally2 tally directory
rationale:
By limiting the number of failed logon attempts, the risk of unauthorized
system access via user password guessing, otherwise known as
brute-force attacks, is reduced. Limits are imposed by locking the account.
ocil_clause: file= is set to /var/log/tallylog or missing
ocil:
Verify the location of the default tallylog file for the pam_tally2 module, with the following command 
$sudo grep -R pam_tally2 /etc/pam.d/login | grep "file=" | grep -v "^#"
fixtext:
Configure SLEM 5 to use the default pam_tally2 tally directory Modify the content of /etc/pam.d/login, like this: 
sudo sed -ri 's/\s+file=\S+\s+/ /g' /etc/pam.d/login
title: SLEM 5 must use the default pam_tally2 tally directory.
